question: How does GROMACS handle SETTLE velocity constraints during CSVR thermostat rescaling? Why does Prolix explode at >10ps while GROMACS works?
findings: CRITICAL: Prolix applies SETTLE velocity constraint BEFORE CSVR thermostat (settle.py:1771–1784 then 1803–1808), creating feedback loop: SETTLE removes KE → CSVR over-rescales → next step SETTLE removes more → exponential divergence
GROMACS applies SETTLE constraints during main integrator loop (before thermostat), decoupling constraint removal from temperature rescaling
Feedback loop magnification weak at dt≤0.5fs (small per-step impulse) but explodes at dt≥1.0fs (cumulative error drives 10^115K explosion)
DOF calculation in Prolix (_n_dof_thermostated line 1131) correctly subtracts 3*N_waters but timing mismatch causes thermostat to see partial/stale KE
Proposed fix: move SETTLE velocity constraint to AFTER CSVR rescaling, or implement KE-loss offset compensation in thermostat target
open_questions: Is rigid_tip3p_box_ke_kcal computing 6*N_waters DOF correctly? (should exclude constrained DOF from kinetic energy count)
Does initial momentum seeding in init_fn (line 1637) respect rigid-body constraints? Standard Maxwell-Boltzmann may seed non-equilibrium KE.
